description: Evidence items for OT security audit — mapped to IEC 62443-3-3 SRs, domain requirements, and NACSA Act 854 obligations. Used by auditors conducting s23 assessments and by OT security teams preparing for audit.
auditorNote: For NACSA s23 audits: the auditor should be NACSA-licensed with OT security expertise (GICSP or equivalent). Evidence should demonstrate both policy (documents exist) and operational effectiveness (controls are working). Passive evidence (logs, monitoring outputs) is more reliable than documentation alone.

network-segmentation.description: Evidence that OT network zones are defined, enforced, and monitored.

evidenceItems.name: Network topology diagram with zones and conduits
whatGoodLooksLike: Current diagram dated within 6 months
All zones labelled with SL-T
All conduits with permitted traffic flows
IDMZ clearly shown breaking IT-OT path
commonGaps: Diagram outdated — doesn't reflect current topology
IDMZ not implemented despite being shown in diagram
evidenceItems.howToVerify: Compare diagram to live network using passive monitoring platform asset discovery

evidenceItems.name: IDMZ firewall rulesets (both firewalls)
whatGoodLooksLike: Deny-all with explicit permit
No direct IT-to-OT Layer 3 routes
All permits have documented business justification
Last reviewed within 6 months
evidenceItems.howToVerify: Review firewall configuration files; test that blocked traffic is actually blocked

evidenceItems.name: OT protocol-aware firewall DPI configuration
whatGoodLooksLike: DPI rules for each OT protocol in use
Function code filtering (e.g., Modbus write blocked where only read expected)
Policy violation log showing DPI is active
commonGaps: DPI licensed but not configured for OT protocols
evidenceItems.howToVerify: Review DPI rule configuration; send test traffic with unexpected function codes and verify block

safety-system.description: Evidence that Safety Instrumented Systems are physically isolated and their integrity is maintained.

evidenceItems.name: SIS network isolation verification record
whatGoodLooksLike: Physical cable trace confirming no network cables between SIS and DCS/IT
Signed off by safety engineer and OT security engineer
Dated within 12 months
commonGaps: Physical access panel connection not traced
Data diode bypassed during maintenance and not restored
evidenceItems.howToVerify: Physical inspection — trace every cable from SIS controller back-panel

evidenceItems.name: SIS program checksum register with pre/post maintenance comparisons
whatGoodLooksLike: Baseline hashes documented at commissioning or last verified state
Pre-maintenance hash matches baseline
Post-maintenance hash matches expectation for approved change
Two-person sign-off on each comparison
commonGaps: Hash comparison not performed after maintenance
evidenceItems.howToVerify: Review checksum register; select one controller and verify current hash against register

evidenceItems.name: SIS key switch configuration records
whatGoodLooksLike: All Triconex/Honeywell SM/ProSafe-RS controllers in Run mode (not Program mode) during normal operation
Key held by named individual — not hanging in cabinet
Log of key switch mode changes
commonGaps: Key switch left in Program mode permanently
No awareness that key switch is a security control
evidenceItems.howToVerify: Physical inspection of all SIS controller key switches; verify key holder identity

incident-response.description: Evidence that the organisation can detect, classify, and respond to OT security incidents — including NACSA s26 notification.

evidenceItems.name: OT monitoring platform deployment and alert records
whatGoodLooksLike: Sensor coverage map showing all OT network segments covered
Alert log showing active detection
Evidence of alert review and triage within defined SLA
NACSA-relevant alert types configured
commonGaps: Monitoring deployed but alerts not reviewed
No OT-specific detection rules — only generic IT rules
evidenceItems.howToVerify: Review sensor coverage map; request sample alert review records for last 30 days

evidenceItems.name: NACSA notification procedure and tabletop exercise records
whatGoodLooksLike: Written procedure with incident classification matrix
NACSA contact details current
Tabletop exercise record showing team classified incidents within 6-hour window
Notification decision authority clearly defined
evidenceItems.howToVerify: Review procedure; confirm NACSA contact details; review most recent tabletop exercise records

evidenceItems.name: OT Incident Response Plan with OT-specific runbooks
whatGoodLooksLike: OT IRP separate from IT IRP or as dedicated OT appendix
System-specific runbooks for key OT scenarios
Operations engineering involvement documented
Annual review and update
commonGaps: IT IRP used as-is for OT — no OT-specific procedures
Operations engineering not involved in OT IR
evidenceItems.howToVerify: Review IRP; check last update date; confirm operations management has reviewed OT sections

asset-management.description: Evidence that OT assets are inventoried, classified, and configuration-managed.

evidenceItems.name: OT asset register completeness evidence
whatGoodLooksLike: All zones represented
Minimum required fields populated (make/model, firmware, IP, zone, criticality)
Last updated date within 30 days
Passive discovery results cross-checked against register — no significant gaps
evidenceItems.howToVerify: Compare register against OT monitoring platform asset list; select 10 random assets and verify register accuracy

evidenceItems.name: OT change management records
whatGoodLooksLike: Change requests for all known changes in period
Post-change verification records
Emergency change records with retrospective review
No gaps between change log and configuration monitoring alerts
commonGaps: Undocumented changes discovered by configuration monitoring
No emergency change process — all changes go through normal process or bypass entirely
evidenceItems.howToVerify: Cross-reference change log with configuration monitoring deviation log; unreconciled deviations indicate gaps
